purpose: The commit fixes a bug where hci_cmd_sync_queue returns an error if HCI_UNREGISTER flag is set. This happens when hci_unregister_dev has been called, which likely causes a use-after-free (UAF) after the timeout as the hdev will be freed.
The commit removes an unnecessary 'asm/termios.h' include in n_hdlc.c. This is a minor code cleanup that does not introduce any new functionality or fix any bugs.
The commit updates documentation for a new metric 'N/A' in store operations.

The commit addresses issues related to memory management in the Linux kernel, specifically around managing huge pages. It introduces a new mechanism for locking VMAs (Virtual Memory Areas) and fixes some potential bugs or race conditions.

The commit introduces two new power supply lines (AVDD and MICVDD) for a Realtek codec (rt5682s). These are already used by sc7180-trogdor-kingoftown.dtsi. The binding file documents these new supplies.

The commit fixes a resource leak in the zorro7xx_remove_one() function by adding an iounmap() call. The error handling path of the probe releases a resource that is not freed in the remove function, and in some cases, a ioremap() must be undone.
The commit removes an unused field from a struct in the mlx5e module of the Linux kernel. The struct is used for asynchronous context operations, and the removed field was not being used.
The commit changes a struct member from unsigned to signed. This change is necessary because it avoids a warning from static checkers such as smatch.
The commit is sorting CONFIG_SURFACE_PLATFORMS in a similar way to savedefconfig. This does not change the functionality of the codebase but rather improves its configuration management.
The commit introduces a new function mcp251xfd_is_fd_mode() to check if the chip's FIFOs are configured for CAN-FD mode. This is done in place of an open coded check.
The commit message states that there is no point in failing the tests when RTC (Real Time Clock) is not present. This implies that the test case should be skipped if the RTC is not available.
The commit is about removing a redundant assignment to variable 'bytes'. The variable 'bytes' is assigned a value that is never read. This seems like a code cleanup or refactoring.
The commit decouples the presence of rcuscale from the presence of RCU Tasks. It seems to be a performance enhancement.
The commit message suggests removing a duplicated word 'be split' in the file spte.h.
The commit reverts a change to populate a clock table based on dcfclk states. The purpose of this change was to prevent an underflow situation when dcfclk states are disabled.
The commit introduces a new GPU to the Linux kernel's device tree bindings for Mali-GPUs. It adds a compatible string for the Renesas RZ/V2L SoC with Bifrost Mali-G31 GPU.
The commit renames a struct from 'pcie_port' to 'dw_pcie_rp'. This is a refactoring change that does not add new functionality or fix bugs.

The commit fixes a clang warning about format specifier mismatch between unsigned short (u16) and int. It also suggests using the %x format specifier instead of %#06hx to avoid any potential issues with integer promotions.

The commit fixes a branch range check issue in MIPS architecture for jump labels. It casts an upper bound of the branch range to long to do signed comparison, which avoids negative offset triggering this warning.
The commit removes a case from SetHwReg8188EU() function that calls another function directly. This suggests it's related to simplifying the code and removing unnecessary parts.
The commit declares a non-global symbol as static. This is done to avoid potential issues related to global symbols in C programming language.
The commit is about removing an incorrect interrupt flags cell from the MDSS nodes in display/msm bindings.
The commit message mentions allocating space for arrays in struct ctrl_ref. This suggests that it is a refactoring or performance improvement related to memory management.

The commit adds support for new hardware platforms to the socinfo driver in the Linux kernel. It specifically mentions SM8350, SC8280XP, SA8540P and one more SM8450 and various PMICs found on boards on these platforms.

The commit recognizes all possible chip cuts (letters from A to P) instead of printing 'unknown' when the cut is greater than E. This change enhances the functionality of the driver by improving its ability to handle different versions of the chip.
The commit removes a custom software enable count from the PRCMU clocks in UX500 platform.
The commit message suggests that it is a refactoring effort to clean up the code related to XIP kernel usage. It also mentions some performance improvements.
The commit enables QUP (Quick Universal Peripheral) and GPI DMA (General Purpose Input Output Direct Memory Access) hardware. This is done to add functioning I2C nodes later.
This commit fixes a typo in comments and replaces an unclear comment with a more descriptive one.
The commit fixes an infinite loop issue in the release_swfw_sync function when failing to acquire a hardware semaphore. The infinite loop may occur if the hardware is surprise-removed.
The commit introduces modern terminology for clocking in the pcm3168a driver.
The commit message suggests removing a pointless hack that is always defined in the code.
This commit implements a feature to respond to GTP echo requests. It adds code that sends an echo response when a GTP packet arrives with the message type of GTP_ECHO_REQ.
The commit is about replacing 'next' versions of cpumask functions with 'first' where appropriate. It also introduces a new function cpumask_first_zero().
The commit removes a call to 'dtim_assoc' IOVAR from the brcmfmac driver. This is done in order to prevent the host driver from trying to get TIM information from firmware after roaming.
This commit adds support for a specific codec (alc5682) on a platform without an amplifier. It appears to be related to Bluetooth audio offload and the Intel SoC.

The commit is about improving the io_uring/net module by adding a check to fail zerocopy sendmsg requests for protocols that don't support it. This is done in order to prevent unsupported operations and improve efficiency.
The commit is about improving the situation by testing existence of xeCJK.sty before loading it. It seems to be a bug fix as it fixes an issue with missing dependencies on some systems.
The commit is about allowing 'case' macros in checkpatch script which checks Linux kernel coding style.

The commit message suggests that it is a refactoring of code to use atomic helpers instead of simple-KMS helpers. The functional changes are minimal and the main purpose seems to be to improve the architecture of the code.
The commit introduces a new interface for jpeg decoding worker in MT8195. It seems to be related to hardware acceleration of image processing.

The commit message indicates that it is removing pointless VPRINTK() calls from the codebase. It also mentions tracepoints which could be used for similar purposes and suggests removing a function ata_scsi_dump_cdb().
The commit adds support for zstate during extended vblank in AMD's display driver. It seems to be related to a feature called Freesync.
The commit fixes a Control Flow Integrity (CFI) failure in the AMDGPU driver by updating the function pointer prototype to match reality. This is done to prevent CFI violations.
The commit corrects a typo in the 'resets' property name in the STM32 pinctrl binding. It also adds missing 'additionalProperties' to ensure strict JSON schema validation.
The commit is about silencing a GCC warning related to array bounds checking in the ath9k driver code.
The commit changes a function call from pm_runtime_get_sync to pm_runtime_resume_and_get. The change is made in the sun8i-ss driver for power management.
The commit is about splitting out fixed file installation and removal into a separate function from the filetable code. It also changes how helpers take context rather than an io_kiocb.
The commit introduces new nodes for G1 and G2 decoders in the device tree bindings. It also updates the power-domain controller to support these separate nodes.
The commit removes a macro that is used only once in another part of the codebase. The removal seems to be related to some kind of refactoring or cleanup.
